title: How do JavaScript closures work?
prompt:
How would you explain JavaScript closures to someone with a knowledge of the concepts they consist of (functions, variables, scope, the like), but does not understand closures themselves?

I have seen the Scheme example given on Wikipedia, but unfortunately it did not help.

Keep your answer accessible — describe what a closure IS, what it consists of, when JavaScript creates one, and why a programmer would care. A short example is welcome but not required to be a deep dive.
